commit_message: Fix for CVE-2015-2141. Thanks to Evgeny Sidorov for reporting. Squaring to satisfy Jacobi requirements suggested by JPM.
cve_desc: The InvertibleRWFunction::CalculateInverse function in rw.cpp in libcrypt++ 5.6.2 does not properly blind private key operations for the Rabin-Williams digital signature algorithm, which allows remote attackers to obtain private keys via a timing attack.

commit_message: Skip MNG CLIP chunk with out-of-range object IDs
cve_desc: In ImageMagick before 6.9.9-0 and 7.x before 7.0.6-1, the ReadOneMNGImage function in coders/png.c has an out-of-bounds read with the MNG CLIP chunk.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Out-of-bounds array access via unchecked loop_level index from LOOP/ENDL chunks
vulnerabilities.long_description: In the LOOP and ENDL chunk handlers, `loop_level` is set directly from `chunk[0]` (a byte value 0-255) without any bounds checking against the size of the `loop_active[]`, `loop_jump[]`, `loop_count[]`, and `loop_iteration[]` arrays in MngInfo. A crafted MNG file with a loop_level value exceeding the array bounds will cause out-of-bounds reads and writes, potentially leading to code execution or a crash.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Heap buffer over-read in TERM chunk due to insufficient length validation
vulnerabilities.long_description: When processing the mng_TERM chunk with `repeat==3`, the code accesses `p[2]` through `p[9]` (via `mng_get_long(&p[2])` and `mng_get_long(&p[6])`). However, the only length check is `if (length != 0)` which merely ensures at least 1 byte. If length is between 1 and 9, this results in a heap buffer over-read, potentially leaking sensitive memory contents or causing a crash.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Out-of-bounds array access in CLIP chunk handler due to unchecked object indices
vulnerabilities.long_description: In the CLIP chunk handler, `first_object` and `last_object` are read from the chunk data and can be up to 65535. The loop iterates from `first_object` to `last_object`, accessing `mng_info->exists[i]`, `mng_info->frozen[i]`, and `mng_info->object_clip[i]` without checking whether `i` is within the bounds of MNG_MAX_OBJECTS. A crafted MNG file can cause out-of-bounds array reads and writes, leading to memory corruption or information disclosure.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Off-by-one error when clamping object_id allows out-of-bounds array access
vulnerabilities.long_description: In the DEFI chunk handler, when `object_id` exceeds `MNG_MAX_OBJECTS`, it is clamped to `MNG_MAX_OBJECTS` instead of `MNG_MAX_OBJECTS-1`. Since the arrays (e.g., `mng_info->exists[]`, `mng_info->frozen[]`, `mng_info->x_off[]`, `mng_info->y_off[]`) are likely declared with size MNG_MAX_OBJECTS, using index MNG_MAX_OBJECTS accesses one element past the end of the array, causing an off-by-one out-of-bounds read/write.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Heap buffer over-read in FRAM chunk due to insufficient bounds checking on pointer advances
vulnerabilities.long_description: In the FRAM chunk handler, after parsing change_delay, change_timeout, and change_clipping flags (advancing p by 4), the code conditionally reads 4-byte longs via `mng_get_long(p)` and advances p further (by 4 for delay, 4 for timeout, 17 for clipping). However, the initial bounds check `(p-chunk) < (ssize_t) (length-4)` does not account for the total bytes that may be consumed by all these conditional reads. Additionally, if `length` is less than 4, `(length-4)` underflows as an unsigned value, making the comparison always true. This can result in reading past the allocated chunk buffer.

commit_message:
Fix for bug #72790 and bug #72799

(cherry picked from commit a14fdb9746262549bbbb96abb87338bacd147e1b)

Conflicts:
	ext/wddx/wddx.c
cve_desc: ext/wddx/wddx.c in PHP before 5.6.25 and 7.x before 7.0.10 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via an invalid wddxPacket XML document that is mishandled in a wddx_deserialize call, as demonstrated by a stray element inside a boolean element, leading to incorrect pop processing.

vulnerabilities.short_description: NULL pointer dereference when php_base64_decode returns NULL
vulnerabilities.long_description: php_base64_decode can return NULL on invalid input or allocation failure. The return value is used directly in ZVAL_STR without a NULL check, leading to a NULL pointer dereference. An attacker can craft a WDDX packet with malformed base64 binary data to trigger a crash.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Unsafe deserialization allows instantiation of arbitrary classes from user-controlled input
vulnerabilities.long_description: The PHP_CLASS_NAME_VAR field from the WDDX data is used to look up and instantiate an arbitrary class via object_init_ex. An attacker can specify any class name in the WDDX input, causing instantiation of arbitrary classes. Combined with the subsequent __wakeup() call and property merging, this enables object injection attacks that can lead to remote code execution, file manipulation, or other impacts depending on available classes and their magic methods.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Potential use-after-free when stack entry is freed but stack not properly cleaned
vulnerabilities.long_description: When Z_TYPE(ent1->data) == IS_UNDEF and stack->top > 1, the code frees ent1 via efree and decrements stack->top, but the pointer in the stack array still references the freed memory. If subsequent operations reference this stack slot (e.g., through a crafted WDDX document that causes the stack to be re-read at this position), it could lead to a use-after-free condition. Similarly the EL_FIELD handling frees the entry but leaves a dangling pointer. An attacker could craft malformed WDDX to trigger memory corruption.

commit_message: Skip MNG CLIP chunk with out-of-range object IDs
cve_desc: In ImageMagick before 6.9.9-0 and 7.x before 7.0.6-1, the ReadOneMNGImage function in coders/png.c has an out-of-bounds read with the MNG CLIP chunk.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Out-of-bounds array access via unchecked loop_level in LOOP chunk
vulnerabilities.long_description: When processing the mng_LOOP chunk, `loop_level` is assigned directly from `chunk[0]` (range 0-255) without any bounds checking against the size of the arrays `loop_active`, `loop_jump`, `loop_count`, and `loop_iteration` in the MngInfo structure. If the chunk byte specifies a value larger than the array dimensions, this leads to out-of-bounds writes, which can corrupt adjacent memory, potentially leading to code execution.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Out-of-bounds array access via unchecked loop_level in ENDL chunk
vulnerabilities.long_description: When processing the mng_ENDL chunk, `loop_level` is assigned from `chunk[0]` without bounds checking. This value is then used to index into `mng_info->loop_active[]`, `mng_info->loop_count[]`, `mng_info->loop_iteration[]`, and `mng_info->loop_jump[]`. An attacker can craft a MNG file with a ENDL chunk byte value exceeding the array bounds, causing out-of-bounds reads and writes.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Heap buffer over-read in TERM chunk due to insufficient length validation
vulnerabilities.long_description: When processing the mng_TERM chunk with repeat==3, the code reads from `&p[2]` (4 bytes) and `&p[6]` (4 bytes), requiring at least 10 bytes of data. However, the only check is `if (length != 0) repeat=p[0]`, meaning a TERM chunk with length as small as 1 would still attempt to read up to 10 bytes from the buffer, causing a heap buffer over-read. An attacker can craft a MNG file with a short TERM chunk to read beyond allocated memory.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Potential buffer over-read in FRAM chunk when scanning frame name
vulnerabilities.long_description: In FRAM chunk processing, after incrementing `p` past the framing mode byte, a while loop scans for a null terminator. If the null byte is exactly at position `length-1` in the chunk, the subsequent `p++` moves `p` to exactly `chunk+length`. The following boundary check `(p-chunk) < (ssize_t)(length-4)` could underflow if length < 4 (since length is size_t, `length-4` wraps to a large value), potentially allowing reads beyond the chunk buffer. Additionally, the subsequent parsing reads multiple fields with pointer increments that may exceed the actual chunk data.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow due to signed shift of unsigned char values
vulnerabilities.long_description: Multiple locations construct 32-bit values by shifting unsigned char values (e.g., `p[0] << 24`). In C, `p[0]` is promoted to `int` before shifting. If `p[0] >= 128`, then `p[0] << 24` overflows a signed 32-bit int, which is undefined behavior per the C standard. This could lead to unexpected values being computed for widths, heights, and offsets, potentially causing subsequent memory corruption.

commit_message: Fix for CVE-2015-2141. Thanks to Evgeny Sidorov for reporting. Squaring to satisfy Jacobi requirements suggested by JPM.
cve_desc: The InvertibleRWFunction::CalculateInverse function in rw.cpp in libcrypt++ 5.6.2 does not properly blind private key operations for the Rabin-Williams digital signature algorithm, which allows remote attackers to obtain private keys via a timing attack.

commit_message:
patch 8.2.4219: reading before the start of the line

Problem:    Reading before the start of the line.
Solution:   Check boundary before trying to read the character.
cve_desc: Heap-based Buffer Overflow in GitHub repository vim/vim prior to 8.2.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow in allocation size can lead to heap buffer overflow
vulnerabilities.long_description: The expression `bd->startspaces + bd->endspaces + bd->textlen + 1` is computed using integer arithmetic and can overflow if the individual values are large enough, resulting in a smaller-than-expected allocation. The subsequent `vim_memset` and `mch_memmove` calls use the original (non-overflowed) sizes, writing past the end of the undersized heap buffer. An attacker who can influence the block selection dimensions (e.g., via a crafted file with extremely long lines or specially crafted visual-block operations) could trigger this to achieve arbitrary heap corruption.

commit_message:
Add more validation to `RequantizationRangePerChannel`.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387693946
Change-Id: Ife8dcbdb021bec4787eef6a4361dd08f17c14bd6
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in MKL implementation of requantization, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantization_range_per_channel_op.cc) does not validate the dimensions of the `input` tensor. A similar issue occurs in `MklRequantizePerChannelOp`. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantize_per_channel_op.cc) does not perform full validation for all the input arguments. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 9e62869465573cb2d9b5053f1fa02a81fce21d69 and in the Github commit 203214568f5bc237603dbab6e1fd389f1572f5c9. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow via std::abs(INT32_MIN) which is undefined behavior
vulnerabilities.long_description: When min_per_channel equals INT32_MIN (-2147483648), std::abs() on a 32-bit signed integer produces undefined behavior because the positive value 2147483648 cannot be represented in int32_t. Depending on the compiler and platform, this can result in INT32_MIN being returned (a negative value), leading to incorrect range calculations, or other unexpected behavior. An attacker who can craft input tensors with values reaching INT32_MIN can trigger this.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Data race on shared boolean variable is_non_negative in OpenMP parallel region
vulnerabilities.long_description: The variable is_non_negative is shared across threads in the OpenMP parallel for loop and is written without any synchronization or atomic operation. Multiple threads may read and write this variable concurrently, constituting a data race and thus undefined behavior per the C++ standard. While the practical impact may be limited since the variable is only ever set from true to false, it is technically UB and a compiler is free to optimize in ways that produce unexpected results. A proper fix would use an OpenMP reduction or atomic operation.

commit_message:
fix possible pattern crash

issue #325
cve_desc: A denial of service vulnerability was found in tildearrow Furnace. It has been classified as problematic. This is due to an incomplete fix of CVE-2022-1211. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely but it requires user interaction. The issue got fixed with the patch 0eb02422d5161767e9983bdaa5c429762d3477ce.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Stack buffer overflow via sprintf into a fixed 32-byte buffer
vulnerabilities.long_description: The static buffer `id` is only 32 bytes, but multiple `sprintf` calls write into it without bounds checking. The format string `"%s##PN_%d_%d"` includes the result of `noteName()` plus two integers, and formats like `"..##PE%d_%d_%d"` or `"%.2X##PF%d_%d_%d"` include three integers. With sufficiently large values of i, j, or k (all derived from song data: pattern length, channel count, and effect rows), the formatted string can exceed 32 bytes. For example, `"..##PE%d_%d_%d"` with k=99, i=9999, j=999 produces a 22-character string, but combined with a longer `noteName()` return value or unexpected integer values, the 32-byte boundary can be overflowed, corrupting adjacent stack/static memory and potentially enabling code execution.

commit_message: fixed #1659
cve_desc: An issue was discovered in GPAC version 0.8.0 and 1.0.1. There is an invalid pointer dereference in the function SetupWriters() in isomedia/isom_store.c.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Memory leak on early return paths when allocation fails
vulnerabilities.long_description: When gf_isom_box_new() fails for stsc or stco, the function returns GF_OUT_OF_MEM directly without freeing the current 'writer' object or calling CleanWriters() on the already-populated 'writers' list. This leaks the current TrackWriter (and its stsc if only stco allocation failed), as well as all previously allocated TrackWriter objects already added to the writers list. The function has a proper 'exit' label with cleanup, but these error paths bypass it.

commit_message: fixed #1782 (fuzz)
cve_desc: Memory leak in the afra_box_read function in MP4Box in GPAC 1.0.1 allows attackers to read memory via a crafted file.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Memory leak when ISOM_DECREASE_SIZE fails after allocating an entry
vulnerabilities.long_description: In both the local and global entry loops, memory is allocated for an entry (ae) via gf_malloc, and then ISOM_DECREASE_SIZE is called which may return an error. If ISOM_DECREASE_SIZE returns an error, the function exits immediately without freeing the just-allocated ae, causing a memory leak. An attacker can craft a malformed box with a large entry_count or global_entry_count but insufficient remaining size, triggering repeated allocation-then-leak cycles, potentially exhausting memory.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Missing size validation for global_entry_count before allocation loop
vulnerabilities.long_description: Unlike entry_count which has a size-based sanity check before its loop, global_entry_count is read and used directly in a loop that allocates memory without any upfront validation against the remaining box size. A crafted file with a very large global_entry_count value could cause many iterations of malloc calls (each allocating a GF_GlobalAfraEntry) before ISOM_DECREASE_SIZE eventually fails, leading to excessive memory consumption. Combined with the memory leak on error paths, this amplifies the impact as all previously allocated entries in that loop iteration are also leaked.

commit_message: Prevent malformed upload path causing arbitrary write (#1174)
cve_desc: This affects the package drogonframework/drogon before 1.7.5. The unsafe handling of file names during upload using HttpFile::save() method may enable attackers to write files to arbitrary locations outside the designated target folder.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Path traversal via user-controlled fileName_ allows writing files outside the intended directory
vulnerabilities.long_description: The member variable fileName_ originates from the uploaded file's filename, which is controlled by the HTTP client. It is used directly in constructing the final save path (fsPath / fsFileName) without any sanitization or validation. An attacker can supply a filename containing path traversal sequences such as "../../etc/cron.d/malicious" to write files to arbitrary locations on the filesystem. This could lead to arbitrary file overwrite, remote code execution (e.g., overwriting configuration files or executables), or denial of service.

commit_message:
Prevent dereferencing of null pointers in TFLite's `add.cc`.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387244946
Change-Id: I56094233327fbd8439b92e1dbb1262176e00eeb9
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions an attacker can craft a TFLite model that would trigger a null pointer dereference, which would result in a crash and denial of service. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/149562d49faa709ea80df1d99fc41d005b81082a/tensorflow/lite/kernels/internal/optimized/optimized_ops.h#L268-L285) unconditionally dereferences a pointer. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 15691e456c7dc9bd6be203b09765b063bf4a380c. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: fixed #1659
cve_desc: An issue was discovered in GPAC version 0.8.0 and 1.0.1. There is an invalid pointer dereference in the function SetupWriters() in isomedia/isom_store.c.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Memory leak on early return paths after allocating TrackWriter
vulnerabilities.long_description: When gf_isom_box_new() fails for writer->stsc or writer->stco, the function returns GF_OUT_OF_MEM directly without freeing the current 'writer' object (and any already-allocated sub-objects like writer->stsc). The writer has not yet been added to the 'writers' list, so CleanWriters() would not free it either. Additionally, any previously added TrackWriter objects in the 'writers' list are also leaked since CleanWriters is not called on these error paths (unlike the 'goto exit' path used when GF_SAFEALLOC fails). This leads to memory leaks that could be triggered repeatedly by crafted input files.

commit_message: https://github.com/ImageMagick/ImageMagick/pull/4986
cve_desc: A vulnerability was found in ImageMagick, causing an outside the range of representable values of type 'unsigned long' at coders/pcl.c, when crafted or untrusted input is processed. This leads to a negative impact to application availability or other problems related to undefined behavior.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Heap buffer overflow due to mismatched buffer size constant (MagickPathExtent vs MaxTextExtent)
vulnerabilities.long_description: The `density` string is allocated via `AcquireString("")`, which in ImageMagick 6.x typically allocates `strlen("") + MaxTextExtent` bytes. However, `FormatLocaleString(density, MagickPathExtent, "2.0x2.0")` uses `MagickPathExtent` as the size limit, which is a constant from ImageMagick 7. If `MagickPathExtent` is larger than the allocated buffer size (MaxTextExtent), this write can overflow the heap buffer. An attacker can trigger this path by crafting a PCL file and setting `image_info->ping` to true, leading to potential heap corruption, code execution, or denial of service.

code_snippets:
(void) FormatLocaleString(command,MaxTextExtent,
    GetDelegateCommands(delegate_info),
    read_info->antialias != MagickFalse ? 4 : 1,
    read_info->antialias != MagickFalse ? 4 : 1,density,options,
    read_info->filename,input_filename);
  ...
  status=ExternalDelegateCommand(MagickFalse,read_info->verbose,command,
    (char *) NULL,exception) != 0 ? MagickTrue : MagickFalse;
vulnerabilities.short_description: OS command injection via shell command constructed from delegate commands format string
vulnerabilities.long_description: The code constructs a shell command by formatting `GetDelegateCommands(delegate_info)` as a printf-style format string with user-influenced arguments (density, options, filenames) and then passes the result to `ExternalDelegateCommand` for shell execution. If the delegate configuration can be influenced (e.g., through policy misconfigurations or if the delegate command template does not properly sanitize/quote arguments), values such as the density string (derived from image resolution metadata in the input file) or filenames could inject arbitrary shell commands. Although some values use unique temporary names, the overall pattern of building shell commands from user-influenced data is inherently dangerous and has been the source of many ImageMagick CVEs.

commit_message: Prevent malformed upload path causing arbitrary write (#1174)
cve_desc: This affects the package drogonframework/drogon before 1.7.5. The unsafe handling of file names during upload using HttpFile::save() method may enable attackers to write files to arbitrary locations outside the designated target folder.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Path traversal allowing file writes to arbitrary locations
vulnerabilities.long_description: The function attempts to constrain relative paths but the check is insufficient. A path like "../../../etc/cron.d/malicious" starts with ".." and bypasses the relative-path prepending, allowing the file to be saved to an arbitrary directory. Similarly, absolute paths are passed through without any sanitization. An attacker who controls the fileName parameter can write uploaded file content to arbitrary filesystem locations, potentially overwriting critical files or achieving code execution.

commit_message:
Secure tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2

Validate size and shape of min_range and max_range
Ensure axis is within input dims limits

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387232799
Change-Id: I36975281f7b5758e9e31a8dcc73fe610ef456318
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in `tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2`, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/84d053187cb80d975ef2b9684d4b61981bca0c41/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_op.cc#L59) has some validation but does not check that `min_range` and `max_range` both have the same non-zero number of elements. If `axis` is provided (i.e., not `-1`), then validation should check that it is a value in range for the rank of `input` tensor and then the lengths of `min_range` and `max_range` inputs match the `axis` dimension of the `input` tensor. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 6da6620efad397c85493b8f8667b821403516708. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
avformat/adtsenc: return value check for init_get_bits in adts_decode_extradata

As the second argument for init_get_bits (buf) can be crafted, a return value check for this function call is necessary.
'buf' is  part of  'AVPacket pkt'.
replace init_get_bits with init_get_bits8.

Signed-off-by: Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
cve_desc: adts_decode_extradata in libavformat/adtsenc.c in FFmpeg 4.4 does not check the init_get_bits return value, which is a necessary step because the second argument to init_get_bits can be crafted.

commit_message:
fix possible pattern crash

issue #325
cve_desc: A denial of service vulnerability was found in tildearrow Furnace. It has been classified as problematic. This is due to an incomplete fix of CVE-2022-1211. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely but it requires user interaction. The issue got fixed with the patch 0eb02422d5161767e9983bdaa5c429762d3477ce.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Stack buffer overflow via sprintf into fixed-size 32-byte buffer
vulnerabilities.long_description: The buffer `id` is only 32 bytes, but multiple `sprintf` calls write to it without bounds checking. The most dangerous is `sprintf(id,"%s##PN_%d_%d",noteName(...),i,j)` where the return value of `noteName` is of unknown length and is concatenated with `##PN_` plus two decimal integers. Other calls like `sprintf(id,"%.2X##PE%d_%d_%d",...)` with three integer parameters (k, i, j) could also approach or exceed 32 bytes if the integers are large. Since `id` is a static local buffer on the stack (despite being declared static, a write past 32 bytes still corrupts adjacent memory), this can lead to memory corruption, potentially enabling code execution.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Potential out-of-bounds read on extFxColors array
vulnerabilities.long_description: When `pat->data[i][index]` is >= 0xe0, the code computes `pat->data[i][index]-0xe0` as an index into `extFxColors`. Since the data values are integers with no upper-bound check beyond being >= 0xe0, if a pattern data value exceeds the expected maximum (e.g., 0xFF for an 8-bit effect, or larger if stored as int), this index can go out of bounds of the `extFxColors` array, resulting in an out-of-bounds read. The read value is then used as an index into `uiColors`, potentially causing a second out-of-bounds access.

commit_message:
crypto: ccp - fix resource leaks in ccp_run_aes_gcm_cmd()

There are three bugs in this code:

1) If we ccp_init_data() fails for &src then we need to free aad.
   Use goto e_aad instead of goto e_ctx.
2) The label to free the &final_wa was named incorrectly as "e_tag" but
   it should have been "e_final_wa".  One error path leaked &final_wa.
3) The &tag was leaked on one error path.  In that case, I added a free
   before the goto because the resource was local to that block.

Fixes: 36cf515b9bbe ("crypto: ccp - Enable support for AES GCM on v5 CCPs")
Reported-by: "minihanshen(沈明航)" <minihanshen@tencent.com>
Signed-off-by: Dan Carpenter <dan.carpenter@oracle.com>
Reviewed-by: John Allen <john.allen@amd.com>
Tested-by: John Allen <john.allen@amd.com>
Signed-off-by: Herbert Xu <herbert@gondor.apana.org.au>
cve_desc: A memory leak flaw was found in the Linux kernel in the ccp_run_aes_gcm_cmd() function in drivers/crypto/ccp/ccp-ops.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption). This vulnerability is similar with the older CVE-2019-18808.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer underflow when src_len is less than authsize in decryption mode
vulnerabilities.long_description: When aes->action is CCP_AES_ACTION_DECRYPT, ilen is computed as aes->src_len - authsize without checking that src_len >= authsize. If src_len is smaller than authsize (both unsigned), this results in an integer underflow producing a very large value. This corrupted ilen is then used to initialize data structures (ccp_init_data), compute scatterlist offsets (scatterwalk_ffwd), and drive loop iterations, potentially leading to out-of-bounds memory access, heap corruption, or denial of service.

replace init_get_bits with init_get_bits8.

commit_message:
patch 8.2.5050: using freed memory when searching for pattern in path

Problem:    Using freed memory when searching for pattern in path.
Solution:   Make a copy of the line.
cve_desc: Use After Free in GitHub repository vim/vim prior to 8.2.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow in allocation size leads to heap buffer overflow
vulnerabilities.long_description: The expression `len + 5` can overflow if `len` (an int parameter) is close to INT_MAX. If `len + 5` wraps to a small positive value or is cast to a large unsigned value that still succeeds, a small buffer is allocated. The subsequent `sprintf` then writes `len` bytes of data into this undersized buffer, causing a heap buffer overflow. An attacker who can control the search pattern length could exploit this to achieve arbitrary code execution.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow when doubling max_path_depth can lead to undersized allocation and out-of-bounds writes
vulnerabilities.long_description: The variable `max_path_depth` starts at 50 and is doubled each time the file stack needs to grow (`max_path_depth *= 2`). After sufficient iterations, `max_path_depth * 2` can overflow the int type, potentially resulting in a small or zero-sized allocation via ALLOC_MULT. Subsequent code then copies `SearchedFile` entries into this undersized buffer using indices based on the non-overflowed logical sizes (old_files, depth), writing out of bounds on the heap. While reaching this requires deeply nested includes, crafted input with circular or deep include chains could trigger it.

code_snippets:
STRNCPY(IObuff + i, aux, p - aux);
i += (int)(p - aux);
vulnerabilities.short_description: Potential buffer over-read and off-by-one when indexing into IObuff
vulnerabilities.long_description: In the ACTION_EXPAND path, after STRNCPY(IObuff, aux, i), accesses like IObuff[i-1] and IObuff[i-2] are performed. If `i` is 0 or 1 (e.g., a very short or empty match), `IObuff[i-2]` reads before the buffer. Additionally, the increments to `i` (appending space, join-space) and subsequent copy into IObuff+i rely on the check `if (p - aux >= IOSIZE - i)` but `i` may have grown past IOSIZE due to the space insertions before this check occurs, potentially writing past the end of IObuff.

commit_message:
crypto: ccp - fix resource leaks in ccp_run_aes_gcm_cmd()

There are three bugs in this code:

1) If we ccp_init_data() fails for &src then we need to free aad.
   Use goto e_aad instead of goto e_ctx.
2) The label to free the &final_wa was named incorrectly as "e_tag" but
   it should have been "e_final_wa".  One error path leaked &final_wa.
3) The &tag was leaked on one error path.  In that case, I added a free
   before the goto because the resource was local to that block.

Fixes: 36cf515b9bbe ("crypto: ccp - Enable support for AES GCM on v5 CCPs")
Reported-by: "minihanshen(沈明航)" <minihanshen@tencent.com>
Signed-off-by: Dan Carpenter <dan.carpenter@oracle.com>
Reviewed-by: John Allen <john.allen@amd.com>
Tested-by: John Allen <john.allen@amd.com>
Signed-off-by: Herbert Xu <herbert@gondor.apana.org.au>
cve_desc: A memory leak flaw was found in the Linux kernel in the ccp_run_aes_gcm_cmd() function in drivers/crypto/ccp/ccp-ops.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption). This vulnerability is similar with the older CVE-2019-18808.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer underflow when src_len is less than authsize in decryption path
vulnerabilities.long_description: When aes->action is CCP_AES_ACTION_DECRYPT (i.e., not ENCRYPT), `ilen` is computed as `aes->src_len - authsize`. Both are unsigned values and there is no check that `aes->src_len >= authsize` before this subtraction. If `aes->src_len` is smaller than `authsize`, the result wraps around to a very large value. This corrupted `ilen` is then used to initialize scatterlist operations (scatterwalk_ffwd, ccp_init_data), potentially leading to out-of-bounds reads/writes, memory corruption, or denial of service.

commit_message:
Fix a null pointer exception caused by branching on uninitialized data.

This is due to not checking that the params for the quantization exists. If there is no quantization, we should not access the `.params` field.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 385163909
Change-Id: I2beb8d50649b6542db224c163033fbcbaa49314f
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions all TFLite operations that use quantization can be made to use unitialized values. [For example](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/lite/kernels/depthwise_conv.cc#L198-L200). The issue stems from the fact that `quantization.params` is only valid if `quantization.type` is different that `kTfLiteNoQuantization`. However, these checks are missing in large parts of the code. We have patched the issue in GitHub commits 537bc7c723439b9194a358f64d871dd326c18887, 4a91f2069f7145aab6ba2d8cfe41be8a110c18a5 and 8933b8a21280696ab119b63263babdb54c298538. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Fix segmentation fault in shape inference logic.

When running shape functions, some functions (such as `MutableHashTableShape`)
produce extra output information in the form of a `ShapeAndType` struct.  The
shapes embedded in this struct are owned by an inference context that is
cleaned up almost immediately; if the upstream code attempts to access this
shape information, it can trigger a segfault.

`ShapeRefiner` is mitigating this for normal output shapes by cloning them
(and thus putting the newly created shape under ownership of an inference
context that will not die), but we were not doing the same for shapes and
types.  This commit fixes that by doing similar logic on output shapes and
types.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 384761124
Change-Id: I07c0c42d29dfbb55bfa13ec1f09ef825fb0a1a1d
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions when running shape functions, some functions (such as `MutableHashTableShape`) produce extra output information in the form of a `ShapeAndType` struct. The shapes embedded in this struct are owned by an inference context that is cleaned up almost immediately; if the upstream code attempts to access this shape information, it can trigger a segfault. `ShapeRefiner` is mitigating this for normal output shapes by cloning them (and thus putting the newly created shape under ownership of an inference context that will not die), but we were not doing the same for shapes and types. This commit fixes that by doing similar logic on output shapes and types. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit ee119d4a498979525046fba1c3dd3f13a039fbb1. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Reorganize and add more validation to MKL requantization

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387901341
Change-Id: I2515b9034c64e113db0bcec8337d30643ab0a0f1
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in MKL implementation of requantization, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantization_range_per_channel_op.cc) does not validate the dimensions of the `input` tensor. A similar issue occurs in `MklRequantizePerChannelOp`. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantize_per_channel_op.cc) does not perform full validation for all the input arguments. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 9e62869465573cb2d9b5053f1fa02a81fce21d69 and in the Github commit 203214568f5bc237603dbab6e1fd389f1572f5c9. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Division by zero when requested_min_max is zero
vulnerabilities.long_description: If both input_requested_min_float and input_requested_max_float are 0.0, then requested_min_max will be 0.0, leading to a floating-point division by zero in the scales computation. This can produce Inf/NaN values that propagate into the MKL-DNN reorder operation, potentially causing undefined behavior or denial of service. An attacker who controls the input tensors can trigger this by supplying zero for both requested min and max inputs.

`ShapeRefiner` is mitigating this for normal output shapes by cloning them
(and thus putting the newly created shape under ownership of an inference
context that will not die), but we were not doing the same for shapes and
types.  This commit fixes that by doing similar logic on output shapes and
types.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 384761124
Change-Id: I07c0c42d29dfbb55bfa13ec1f09ef825fb0a1a1d

This commit fixes that by doing similar logic on output shapes and types. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit ee119d4a498979525046fba1c3dd3f13a039fbb1. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: fixed #1885
cve_desc: Segmentation fault vulnerability exists in Gpac through 1.0.1 via the gf_odf_size_descriptor function in desc_private.c when using mp4box, which causes a denial of service.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Stack-based buffer overflow when base64-encoded data is formatted into sdpLine via sprintf
vulnerabilities.long_description: buf64 is 5000 bytes and sdpLine is 5100 bytes. The gf_base64_encode calls use an output limit of 2000 but buf64 is 5000 bytes; if the limit is not strictly enforced or if the encoded IOD descriptor (whose size is not bounded) produces more output, buf64 could contain up to ~5000 characters. When sprintf formats buf64 into sdpLine with a prefix like 'a=mpeg4-iod:"data:application/mpeg4-iod;base64,' (about 47 characters plus a closing quote), the total can exceed sdpLine's 5100-byte stack buffer, causing a stack buffer overflow. This could allow code execution or denial of service.

vulnerabilities.short_description: NULL pointer dereference when gf_isom_get_esd returns NULL for the scene track
vulnerabilities.long_description: The return value of gf_isom_get_esd(file, sceneT, 1) is never checked for NULL before being dereferenced. If the function returns NULL (e.g., if the scene track has no ESD), the subsequent accesses to esd->OCRESID and esd->ESID will dereference a NULL pointer, leading to a crash. Similarly, esd is added to iod->ESDescriptors even if NULL. The same pattern exists for the OD track's esd when odT is non-zero and gf_isom_get_esd returns NULL — the code dereferences esd->OCRESID without a NULL check.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Use of potentially NULL esd pointer after OD track sample processing
vulnerabilities.long_description: When odT is non-zero, esd is obtained from gf_isom_get_esd. If this returns NULL, the code later dereferences esd->OCRESID without any NULL check. Even if the esd was valid, the code flow is fragile — esd could be NULL if the track configuration is unexpected, leading to a NULL pointer dereference and crash.

commit_message:
Validate that matrix dimension sizes in SparseMatMul are positive.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 401149683
Change-Id: Ib33eafc561a39c8741ece80b2edce6d4aae9a57d
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the code for sparse matrix multiplication is vulnerable to undefined behavior via binding a reference to `nullptr`. This occurs whenever the dimensions of `a` or `b` are 0 or less. In the case on one of these is 0, an empty output tensor should be allocated (to conserve the invariant that output tensors are always allocated when the operation is successful) but nothing should be written to it (that is, we should return early from the kernel implementation). Otherwise, attempts to write to this empty tensor would result in heap OOB access. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Fix tf.raw_ops.DeleteSessionTensor vulnerability with invalid `handle`.

Check that `handle` input is actually a scalar before treating it as such.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 445228994
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. Prior to versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4, the implementation of `tf.raw_ops.DeleteSessionTensor` does not fully validate the input arguments. This results in a `CHECK`-failure which can be used to trigger a denial of service attack. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

commit_message: Fix handling of APNG with 0 delay_den (#313)
cve_desc: libjxl v0.3.7 is affected by a Divide By Zero in issue in lib/extras/codec_apng.cc jxl::DecodeImageAPNG(). When encoding a malicous APNG file using cjxl, an attacker can trigger a denial of service.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow in image buffer size calculation leads to heap buffer overflow
vulnerabilities.long_description: The variables `rowbytes` (w * 4) and `imagesize` (h * rowbytes) are unsigned int (32-bit). If w and h are large but still within `cMaxPNGSize`, these multiplications can overflow, resulting in a much smaller allocation than intended. Subsequent writes into `frameRaw.p` via the row pointers will write far beyond the allocated buffer, causing a heap buffer overflow. An attacker can craft a PNG with large width/height values to trigger this and potentially achieve arbitrary code execution.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Division by zero when delay_den read from fcTL chunk is zero
vulnerabilities.long_description: The `delay_den` value is read directly from the fcTL chunk data without any validation that it is non-zero. It is subsequently used as a divisor in `delay_num * 1000 / delay_den`. A crafted APNG file with delay_den set to 0 will cause a division by zero, crashing the application (denial of service).

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow in dec_pixels accumulation
vulnerabilities.long_description: The multiplication `w0 * h0` is performed on unsigned int values and accumulated into `dec_pixels` across multiple frames. This can overflow, potentially bypassing any downstream pixel count limits intended to prevent resource exhaustion or overly large decodes.

This results in a `CHECK`-failure which can be used to trigger a denial of service attack. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

commit_message:
Fixed typo while calculating module path length.

The issue was introduced in 77c398f26d7e (not released yet).
cve_desc: Nginx NJS v0.7.3 was discovered to contain a stack overflow in the function njs_default_module_loader at /src/njs/src/njs_module.c. NOTE: multiple third parties dispute this report, e.g., the behavior is only found in unreleased development code that was not part of the 0.7.2, 0.7.3, or 0.7.4 release

vulnerabilities.short_description: Stack buffer overflow due to incomplete length check when dir is non-NULL
vulnerabilities.long_description: When `dir` is non-NULL, `length` is set to `dir->length` (+ 1 for a trailing slash), but `info->name.length` is not added to it. The subsequent bounds check `length > NJS_MAX_PATH` therefore only validates the directory portion, not the full combined path. The function then copies both `dir` and `info->name` into the stack buffer `src[NJS_MAX_PATH + 1]`. An attacker who controls `info->name` can provide a long module name that, combined with a short directory, exceeds NJS_MAX_PATH and overflows the stack buffer, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution.

commit_message:
Fixed typo while calculating module path length.

The issue was introduced in 77c398f26d7e (not released yet).
cve_desc: Nginx NJS v0.7.3 was discovered to contain a stack overflow in the function njs_default_module_loader at /src/njs/src/njs_module.c. NOTE: multiple third parties dispute this report, e.g., the behavior is only found in unreleased development code that was not part of the 0.7.2, 0.7.3, or 0.7.4 release

vulnerabilities.short_description: Out-of-bounds read when dir->length is 0 but info->name.length is non-zero
vulnerabilities.long_description: When `dir` is not NULL but `dir->length` is 0, and `info->name.length` is greater than 0, the combined `length` will be non-zero, so the `NJS_DECLINED` early return is not taken. The code then evaluates `dir->start[dir->length - 1]`, which becomes `dir->start[(size_t)-1]` (or `dir->start[-1]` if signed), reading memory before the `dir->start` buffer. This is a heap or stack out-of-bounds read that could leak sensitive data or cause a crash.

commit_message:
Fix FPE issue with `tf.raw_ops.Reverse`.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 371176973
Change-Id: Ic6d483bfc95313ec2299c2d1c956cfe96c96626c
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. An attacker can cause a denial of service via a FPE runtime error in `tf.raw_ops.Reverse`. This is because the implementation(https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/36229ea9e9451dac14a8b1f4711c435a1d84a594/tensorflow/core/kernels/reverse_op.cc#L75-L76) performs a division based on the first dimension of the tensor argument. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.5.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.4.2, TensorFlow 2.3.3, TensorFlow 2.2.3 and TensorFlow 2.1.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: fixed #1885
cve_desc: Segmentation fault vulnerability exists in Gpac through 1.0.1 via the gf_odf_size_descriptor function in desc_private.c when using mp4box, which causes a denial of service.

vulnerabilities.short_description: NULL pointer dereference when gf_isom_get_esd returns NULL for the scene track
vulnerabilities.long_description: The return value of gf_isom_get_esd(file, sceneT, 1) is never checked for NULL before being dereferenced. If the function returns NULL (e.g., when the ESD cannot be retrieved for the scene track), the subsequent accesses to esd->OCRESID and esd->ESID, as well as gf_list_add(iod->ESDescriptors, esd), will dereference a NULL pointer, leading to a crash. This can be triggered by providing a crafted MP4 file where the scene track exists but has no valid ESD.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Stack buffer overflow via base64-encoded data written into fixed-size buffers with sprintf
vulnerabilities.long_description: The buf64 buffer is 5000 bytes but gf_base64_encode is called with a limit of 2000. If gf_base64_encode does not properly enforce the output limit (or the limit refers to input rather than output), the base64 output for large sample data could overflow buf64. Furthermore, even if the base64 output stays within bounds, the subsequent sprintf calls concatenate the base64 string with prefix/suffix strings into sdpLine (5100 bytes) without length checks. A sufficiently large IOD descriptor or sample data could cause the formatted string to exceed 5100 bytes, resulting in a stack-based buffer overflow. An attacker could craft a malicious MP4 file to trigger this and potentially achieve code execution.

vulnerabilities.short_description: NULL pointer dereference on esd in OD track handling
vulnerabilities.long_description: When processing the OD track, gf_isom_get_esd(file, odT, 1) is called but its return value is never checked for NULL. If NULL is returned, subsequent dereferences such as esd->decoderConfig, esd->OCRESID, and esd->URLString will cause a NULL pointer dereference and crash. A crafted file with an OD track but invalid/missing ESD could trigger this.

commit_message:
Fix FPE issue with `tf.raw_ops.Reverse`.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 371176973
Change-Id: Ic6d483bfc95313ec2299c2d1c956cfe96c96626c
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. An attacker can cause a denial of service via a FPE runtime error in `tf.raw_ops.Reverse`. This is because the implementation(https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/36229ea9e9451dac14a8b1f4711c435a1d84a594/tensorflow/core/kernels/reverse_op.cc#L75-L76) performs a division based on the first dimension of the tensor argument. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.5.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.4.2, TensorFlow 2.3.3, TensorFlow 2.2.3 and TensorFlow 2.1.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Fix NPE in restoring code.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 388303253
Change-Id: Ia8c68568cb854bca538909a182b31a618d68ce55
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. When restoring tensors via raw APIs, if the tensor name is not provided, TensorFlow can be tricked into dereferencing a null pointer. Alternatively, attackers can read memory outside the bounds of heap allocated data by providing some tensor names but not enough for a successful restoration. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/47a06f40411a69c99f381495f490536972152ac0/tensorflow/core/kernels/save_restore_tensor.cc#L158-L159) retrieves the tensor list corresponding to the `tensor_name` user controlled input and immediately retrieves the tensor at the restoration index (controlled via `preferred_shard` argument). This occurs without validating that the provided list has enough values. If the list is empty this results in dereferencing a null pointer (undefined behavior). If, however, the list has some elements, if the restoration index is outside the bounds this results in heap OOB read. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 9e82dce6e6bd1f36a57e08fa85af213e2b2f2622. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Reorganize and add more validation to MKL requantization

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387901341
Change-Id: I2515b9034c64e113db0bcec8337d30643ab0a0f1
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in MKL implementation of requantization, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantization_range_per_channel_op.cc) does not validate the dimensions of the `input` tensor. A similar issue occurs in `MklRequantizePerChannelOp`. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantize_per_channel_op.cc) does not perform full validation for all the input arguments. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 9e62869465573cb2d9b5053f1fa02a81fce21d69 and in the Github commit 203214568f5bc237603dbab6e1fd389f1572f5c9. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Wrong variable checked in validation for input_requested_max (copy-paste bug)
vulnerabilities.long_description: The validation for input_requested_max incorrectly checks input_requested_min.NumElements() == 1 instead of input_requested_max.NumElements(). Since input_requested_min was already validated to have 1 element, this check always passes, allowing input_requested_max to have an arbitrary number of elements. If input_requested_max has 0 elements, the subsequent access input_requested_max.flat<float>()(0) would be an out-of-bounds read.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Division by zero when both requested min and max are zero
vulnerabilities.long_description: If both input_requested_min_float and input_requested_max_float are 0.0f, then requested_min_max will be 0.0f, causing a floating-point division by zero in the scales computation. While for QINT8 output type, input_requested_min_float must be negative, for QUINT8 output type there is no such constraint, allowing both values to be zero. This could lead to inf/NaN values propagating through the computation.

vulnerabilities.short_description: No validation that depth matches the actual channel dimension of the 4D input tensor
vulnerabilities.long_description: The depth variable is derived from input_min_vec.NumElements() but is never validated against the actual depth/channel dimension of the 4D input tensor. If an attacker provides an input_min_vec with more elements than the actual channel dimension, the scales vector will be oversized and the reorder operation may read out of bounds from the input tensor or write out of bounds on the output tensor. Conversely, if it has fewer elements, the per-channel scaling will be incomplete.

commit_message: fixed #2212
cve_desc: Use After Free in GitHub repository gpac/gpac prior to 2.1-DEV.

commit_message:
Prevent unitialized variable use in grappler.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 399702928
Change-Id: Id7e75451fbff297692dfb687f60ea04b25c96b24
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions TensorFlow's Grappler optimizer has a use of unitialized variable. If the `train_nodes` vector (obtained from the saved model that gets optimized) does not contain a `Dequeue` node, then `dequeue_node` is left unitialized. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Use of uninitialized pointer `dequeue_node` when no dequeue op is found
vulnerabilities.long_description: The pointer `dequeue_node` is declared without initialization. If no node in `train_nodes` satisfies `IsDequeueOp`, `dequeue_node` remains uninitialized. The subsequent `if (dequeue_node)` check reads an indeterminate value, which is undefined behavior. Depending on the garbage value, the code may dereference a wild pointer (e.g., calling `dequeue_node->name()`), leading to crashes or potentially exploitable memory corruption. Additionally, even if the `if` block is skipped, `dequeue_node` is later used unconditionally in the comparison `input_node->name() != dequeue_node->name()` within the `input_nodes` loop, though that loop would be empty in this path. Initializing the pointer to `nullptr` would fix the UB.

commit_message:
Fix NPE in restoring code.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 388303253
Change-Id: Ia8c68568cb854bca538909a182b31a618d68ce55
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. When restoring tensors via raw APIs, if the tensor name is not provided, TensorFlow can be tricked into dereferencing a null pointer. Alternatively, attackers can read memory outside the bounds of heap allocated data by providing some tensor names but not enough for a successful restoration. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/47a06f40411a69c99f381495f490536972152ac0/tensorflow/core/kernels/save_restore_tensor.cc#L158-L159) retrieves the tensor list corresponding to the `tensor_name` user controlled input and immediately retrieves the tensor at the restoration index (controlled via `preferred_shard` argument). This occurs without validating that the provided list has enough values. If the list is empty this results in dereferencing a null pointer (undefined behavior). If, however, the list has some elements, if the restoration index is outside the bounds this results in heap OOB read. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 9e82dce6e6bd1f36a57e08fa85af213e2b2f2622. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Secure tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2

Validate size and shape of min_range and max_range
Ensure axis is within input dims limits

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387232799
Change-Id: I36975281f7b5758e9e31a8dcc73fe610ef456318
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in `tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2`, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/84d053187cb80d975ef2b9684d4b61981bca0c41/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_op.cc#L59) has some validation but does not check that `min_range` and `max_range` both have the same non-zero number of elements. If `axis` is provided (i.e., not `-1`), then validation should check that it is a value in range for the rank of `input` tensor and then the lengths of `min_range` and `max_range` inputs match the `axis` dimension of the `input` tensor. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 6da6620efad397c85493b8f8667b821403516708. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Missing validation of axis_ against input tensor dimensions leads to out-of-bounds access
vulnerabilities.long_description: The code uses axis_ to index into the input tensor's dimensions via input.dim_size(axis_) without first checking that axis_ is less than input.dims(). If axis_ is greater than or equal to the number of dimensions of the input tensor, this results in an out-of-bounds read. An attacker who can control the axis attribute and the input tensor shape could trigger undefined behavior or a crash.

vulnerabilities.short_description: No validation that min/max range tensors have num_slices elements, leading to out-of-bounds read
vulnerabilities.long_description: The loop iterates num_slices times and accesses min_ranges(i) and max_ranges(i), but there is no check that input_min_range and input_max_range tensors actually contain at least num_slices elements. If an attacker provides min/max range tensors with fewer elements than the number of slices along the given axis, the indexed accesses will read out of bounds, potentially leaking memory contents or causing a crash.

commit_message:
The code now checks for consistency of the MCU sizes across
hierarchical levels, and fails in case they are different.
cve_desc: libjpeg 1.63 has a heap-based buffer over-read in HierarchicalBitmapRequester::FetchRegion in hierarchicalbitmaprequester.cpp because the MCU size can be different between allocation and use.

commit_message:
Fix EinsumHelper::ParseEquation to avoid uninitialized accesses.

EinsumHelper::ParseEquation is supposed to return true or false in
input_has_ellipsis and output_has_ellipsis to indicate whether there is
ellipsis in the inputs and output. Previously, when there is no ellipsis in the
inputs or output, the routine doesn't assign false to the variables. This
change initializes the two variables with false to fix the problem.
PiperOrigin-RevId: 391772004
Change-Id: I17b6c88aadef4131470378e48cced054bf252e86
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affeced versions during execution, `EinsumHelper::ParseEquation()` is supposed to set the flags in `input_has_ellipsis` vector and `*output_has_ellipsis` boolean to indicate whether there is ellipsis in the corresponding inputs and output. However, the code only changes these flags to `true` and never assigns `false`. This results in unitialized variable access if callers assume that `EinsumHelper::ParseEquation()` always sets these flags. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Out-of-bounds read when num_inputs is 0 or greater than 2
vulnerabilities.long_description: When num_inputs is 0, the short-circuit evaluation of `num_inputs == 1` yields false, causing accesses to `(*input_label_counts)[0]` and `(*input_label_counts)[1]` on an empty vector. Similarly, if num_inputs is exactly 1, the first condition short-circuits, but if num_inputs is any value other than 1 (e.g., 0 or 3+), the code unconditionally accesses index 1, which may be out of bounds since `input_label_counts` is resized to `num_inputs`. An attacker who can control the einsum equation string (e.g., providing zero inputs or more than two) could trigger an out-of-bounds memory read, potentially leading to a crash (denial of service) or information disclosure.

commit_message:
Prevent unitialized variable use in grappler.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 399702928
Change-Id: Id7e75451fbff297692dfb687f60ea04b25c96b24
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions TensorFlow's Grappler optimizer has a use of unitialized variable. If the `train_nodes` vector (obtained from the saved model that gets optimized) does not contain a `Dequeue` node, then `dequeue_node` is left unitialized. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Use-after-free via invalidated pointers when protobuf repeated field reallocates after adding nodes to the graph.
vulnerabilities.long_description: The code first populates `all_nodes_` with raw pointers obtained from `graph_.mutable_node(i)`. These pointers point into the internal storage of the protobuf `RepeatedPtrField<NodeDef>`. Subsequently, `AddNodeDivConst()` and `AddNodeDiv()` add new nodes to `graph_`, which can trigger a reallocation of the underlying repeated field, invalidating all previously stored pointers. The code then dereferences these stale pointers (e.g., `all_nodes_[apply_gradient_node_name]->op()` and `->input(...)` and `->mutable_input(...)`), leading to use-after-free. An attacker who can influence the graph structure (e.g., by crafting a model with specific apply-gradient operations) could potentially exploit this to achieve arbitrary read/write in memory.

commit_message: Make line_no with too large value(2**20) invalid. Fixes #124
cve_desc: tinyexr 0.9.5 has a integer overflow over-write in tinyexr::DecodePixelData in tinyexr.h, related to OpenEXR code.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer overflow in offset bounds checks can bypass validation and cause out-of-bounds reads
vulnerabilities.long_description: The expressions `offsets[tile_idx] + sizeof(int) * 5` and `offsets[y_idx] + sizeof(int) * 2` can wrap around if the offset value is close to SIZE_MAX. This would cause the bounds check against `size` to pass even though the offset is far beyond the buffer, leading to out-of-bounds memory reads when `data_ptr` is computed as `head + offsets[...]`. An attacker can craft a malicious EXR file with large offset values to trigger this.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Missing NULL check after calloc for tiles can lead to NULL pointer dereference
vulnerabilities.long_description: The calloc call for `exr_image->tiles` does not check for a NULL return value. If memory allocation fails (e.g., due to a very large `num_tiles` value derived from attacker-controlled offset data), the subsequent loop will dereference a NULL pointer, causing a crash or potentially exploitable behavior.

code_snippets: bool invalid_data = false;  // TODO(LTE): Use atomic lock for MT safety.
vulnerabilities.short_description: Data race on `invalid_data` flag in OpenMP parallel region
vulnerabilities.long_description: The `invalid_data` boolean is read and written by multiple OpenMP threads concurrently without any synchronization or atomic operations. This is a data race (undefined behavior in C++). One thread may set it to true while another thread reads a stale value and continues processing invalid data, potentially leading to heap corruption or out-of-bounds memory access in DecodePixelData.

code_snippets:
if (!tinyexr::DecodePixelData(
                      exr_image->images, exr_header->requested_pixel_types,
                      data_ptr, static_cast<size_t>(data_len),
                      exr_header->compression_type, exr_header->line_order,
                      data_width, data_height, data_width, y, line_no,
                      num_lines, static_cast<size_t>(pixel_data_size),
vulnerabilities.short_description: Insufficient validation of line_no against data_height may cause out-of-bounds write in DecodePixelData
vulnerabilities.long_description: While `line_no` is checked to be non-negative after adjustment, there is no check that `line_no + num_lines <= data_height`. The image buffer is allocated for `data_width * data_height` pixels, but a crafted file could provide a `line_no` value that, while positive, exceeds the allocated buffer bounds. This could allow DecodePixelData to write beyond the allocated image buffer, resulting in heap buffer overflow. Additionally, `line_no + num_scanline_blocks` in the end_line_no computation can overflow for large line_no values.

commit_message:
The code now checks for consistency of the MCU sizes across
hierarchical levels, and fails in case they are different.
cve_desc: libjpeg 1.63 has a heap-based buffer over-read in HierarchicalBitmapRequester::FetchRegion in hierarchicalbitmaprequester.cpp because the MCU size can be different between allocation and use.

commit_message:
Merge pull request from GHSA-vhxv-phmx-g52q

* Prevent OOB read/write when parsing RTCP FB RPSI

* Add log information

* Modification based on comments.
cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C. PJSIP versions 2.12 and prior do not parse incoming RTCP feedback RPSI (Reference Picture Selection Indication) packet, but any app that directly uses pjmedia_rtcp_fb_parse_rpsi() will be affected. A patch is available in the `master` branch of the `pjsip/pjproject` GitHub repository. There are currently no known workarounds.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer underflow in rpsi_bit_len due to unchecked attacker-controlled padlen
vulnerabilities.long_description: The `padlen` value is read directly from the packet data without validation. If an attacker crafts a packet where `padlen` is greater than `rpsi_len*8 - 16`, the unsigned subtraction `rpsi_len*8 - 16 - padlen` underflows, producing a very large value for `rpsi_bit_len`. This huge value is then used to compute the string length passed to `pj_strset` as `(rpsi->rpsi_bit_len + 7)/8`, resulting in the `rpsi->rpsi` string referencing memory far beyond the actual buffer. Any subsequent code that reads this string will perform a massive out-of-bounds read, potentially leaking sensitive memory contents or causing a crash.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Potential integer underflow when hdr->length is less than 2
vulnerabilities.long_description: If the RTCP header's `length` field (after byte-order conversion) is 0 or 1, the expression `(pj_ntohs((pj_uint16_t)hdr->length)-2)` underflows. Due to integer promotion rules, this yields a negative value which, when multiplied by 4 and assigned to the unsigned `pj_size_t rpsi_len`, produces a very large number. While the subsequent `length < rpsi_len + 12` check will likely catch this on 64-bit platforms, on 32-bit platforms `rpsi_len + 12` could itself overflow back to a small value, potentially bypassing the bounds check and allowing out-of-bounds memory access.

commit_message:
Fixed frame allocation from an awaited frame.

njs_function_frame_save() is used to save the awaited frame when "await"
instruction is encountered. The saving was done as a memcpy() of
existing runtime frame.

njs_function_frame_alloc() is used to alloc a new function frame, this
function tries to use a spare preallocated memory from the previous
frame first.  Previously, this function might result in "use-after-free"
when invoked from a restored frame saved with njs_function_frame_save().
Because njs_function_frame_save() left pointers to the spare memory of
the original frame which may be already free when saved frame is
restored.

The fix is to erase fields for the spare memory from the saved frame.

This closes #469 issue on Github.
cve_desc: nginx njs 0.7.2 is affected suffers from Use-after-free in njs_function_frame_alloc() when it try to invoke from a restored frame saved with njs_function_frame_save().

commit_message:
Fix EinsumHelper::ParseEquation to avoid uninitialized accesses.

EinsumHelper::ParseEquation is supposed to return true or false in
input_has_ellipsis and output_has_ellipsis to indicate whether there is
ellipsis in the inputs and output. Previously, when there is no ellipsis in the
inputs or output, the routine doesn't assign false to the variables. This
change initializes the two variables with false to fix the problem.
PiperOrigin-RevId: 391772004
Change-Id: I17b6c88aadef4131470378e48cced054bf252e86
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affeced versions during execution, `EinsumHelper::ParseEquation()` is supposed to set the flags in `input_has_ellipsis` vector and `*output_has_ellipsis` boolean to indicate whether there is ellipsis in the corresponding inputs and output. However, the code only changes these flags to `true` and never assigns `false`. This results in unitialized variable access if callers assume that `EinsumHelper::ParseEquation()` always sets these flags. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Out-of-bounds read when num_inputs is 0 or only index 0 and 1 are checked for num_inputs > 2
vulnerabilities.long_description: When `num_inputs` is 0 (i.e., `input_str` is empty after parsing), the short-circuit evaluation of `num_inputs == 1` evaluates to false, and the code proceeds to access `(*input_label_counts)[0][label]`, which is an out-of-bounds read on an empty vector. Additionally, when `num_inputs > 2`, only indices 0 and 1 of `input_label_counts` are consulted, silently ignoring other inputs — though this is more of a logic bug. The `num_inputs == 0` case is a concrete out-of-bounds memory access that could lead to a crash or information disclosure if an attacker can control the einsum equation string to produce zero input operands.

commit_message:
Fixed frame allocation from an awaited frame.

njs_function_frame_save() is used to save the awaited frame when "await"
instruction is encountered. The saving was done as a memcpy() of
existing runtime frame.

njs_function_frame_alloc() is used to alloc a new function frame, this
function tries to use a spare preallocated memory from the previous
frame first.  Previously, this function might result in "use-after-free"
when invoked from a restored frame saved with njs_function_frame_save().
Because njs_function_frame_save() left pointers to the spare memory of
the original frame which may be already free when saved frame is
restored.

The fix is to erase fields for the spare memory from the saved frame.

This closes #469 issue on Github.
cve_desc: nginx njs 0.7.2 is affected suffers from Use-after-free in njs_function_frame_alloc() when it try to invoke from a restored frame saved with njs_function_frame_save().

commit_message:
Fix tf.raw_ops.QuantizeAndDequantizeV4Grad vulnerability with invalid input_min or input_max.

Check that argument is actually a scalar before treating it as such.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 445198280
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. Prior to versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4, the implementation of `tf.raw_ops.QuantizeAndDequantizeV4Grad` does not fully validate the input arguments. This results in a `CHECK`-failure which can be used to trigger a denial of service attack. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

vulnerabilities.short_description: When axis_ is 0, `axis_ - 1` equals -1, causing an invalid argument to flat_inner_outer_dims leading to undefined behavior or crash.
vulnerabilities.long_description: The validation permits `axis_` to be 0 (the check is `axis_ == -1 || axis_ < input.shape().dims()`). When `axis_` is 0, the code enters the `else` branch (since `axis_ != -1`) and calls `flat_inner_outer_dims<T, 3>(axis_ - 1)` which resolves to `flat_inner_outer_dims<T, 3>(-1)`. The `flat_inner_outer_dims` function expects a non-negative dimension index. Passing -1 can result in out-of-bounds memory access, a CHECK failure/crash, or other undefined behavior. An attacker can trigger this by providing axis=0 as an attribute, causing a denial of service.

commit_message:
Ensure non-empty rt_nested_splits in tf.raw_ops.RaggedTensorToVariant

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387664237
Change-Id: Ia1700c34b5610873d63561abc86e23b46ead93b3
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions an attacker can cause undefined behavior via binding a reference to null pointer in `tf.raw_ops.RaggedTensorToVariant`. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/ragged_tensor_to_variant_op.cc#L129) has an incomplete validation of the splits values, missing the case when the argument would be empty. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit be7a4de6adfbd303ce08be4332554dff70362612. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Fix tf.raw_ops.QuantizeAndDequantizeV4Grad vulnerability with invalid input_min or input_max.

Check that argument is actually a scalar before treating it as such.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 445198280
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. Prior to versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4, the implementation of `tf.raw_ops.QuantizeAndDequantizeV4Grad` does not fully validate the input arguments. This results in a `CHECK`-failure which can be used to trigger a denial of service attack. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Integer underflow when axis_ is 0 leads to invalid memory access in flat_inner_outer_dims
vulnerabilities.long_description: When axis_ is 0, the expression `axis_ - 1` evaluates to -1, which is then passed to `flat_inner_outer_dims<T, 3>()`. This function expects a non-negative dimension index. Passing -1 can cause out-of-bounds read/write or other undefined behavior within the Eigen tensor reshaping logic. The validation only checks `axis_ >= -1` and `axis_ < input.shape().dims()`, so axis_=0 passes all checks but triggers the bug in the else branch.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Accessing dim_size(0) on a scalar (0-dimensional) tensor when axis_ != -1
vulnerabilities.long_description: When axis_ != -1, the code calls `input_min_tensor.dim_size(0)` and `input_max_tensor.dim_size(0)`. However, the earlier validation allows these tensors to have `dims() == 0` (i.e., scalar tensors). Calling `dim_size(0)` on a 0-dimensional tensor is an out-of-bounds access on the shape's dimension array, which can lead to undefined behavior including reading garbage values or crashing.

commit_message: fixed #2212
cve_desc: Use After Free in GitHub repository gpac/gpac prior to 2.1-DEV.

vulnerabilities.short_description: Untrusted bitstream value used for large memory allocation without bounds checking
vulnerabilities.long_description: NbBits is read as a 5-bit value (0–31) and then nbFields is read using NbBits bits, meaning nbFields can be up to 2^31 - 1. This attacker-controlled value is passed directly to gf_sg_vrml_mf_alloc to allocate an array, and is also used as the loop bound for iterating and decoding fields. A crafted bitstream can specify an extremely large nbFields value, causing excessive memory allocation (denial of service) or, if the element size multiplied by nbFields overflows internally, a heap buffer overflow when the loop writes beyond the undersized allocation.

commit_message:
Make Transpose's shape inference function validate that negative `perm` values are within the tensor's rank.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 403252853
Change-Id: Ia6b31b45b237312668bb31c2c3b3c7bbce2d2610
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the shape inference function for `Transpose` is vulnerable to a heap buffer overflow. This occurs whenever `perm` contains negative elements. The shape inference function does not validate that the indices in `perm` are all valid. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Prevent heap OOB read in TFLite's `gather_nd.cc`.

Passing negative indices is illegal but there was a missing check so that resulted in OOB accesses.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 387208551
Change-Id: I6b7a8a62d3e7c13a16d81619e5bc23ae2cdbc7fd
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions TFLite's [`GatherNd` implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/149562d49faa709ea80df1d99fc41d005b81082a/tensorflow/lite/kernels/gather_nd.cc#L124) does not support negative indices but there are no checks for this situation. Hence, an attacker can read arbitrary data from the heap by carefully crafting a model with negative values in `indices`. Similar issue exists in [`Gather` implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/149562d49faa709ea80df1d99fc41d005b81082a/tensorflow/lite/kernels/gather.cc). We have patched the issue in GitHub commits bb6a0383ed553c286f87ca88c207f6774d5c4a8f and eb921122119a6b6e470ee98b89e65d721663179d. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.
